key_models.ai.agent: AI agent definition. Fields: name, system_prompt, welcome_message, model_id, provider_id, tool_ids, visibility, active, memory_enabled, max_history, handoff_threshold, user_id (bot user). Inherits: mail.thread, mail.activity.mixin.
key_models.ai.provider: LLM provider configuration. Fields: name, provider_type (openai, anthropic, azure_openai, ollama, groq, custom), api_key (encrypted), base_url, timeout, max_retries, active, company_id.
key_models.ai.model: LLM model registry. Fields: name, provider_id, model_name, context_window, input_price, output_price, modality (text, image, audio), active, capability_tags.
key_models.ai.agent.session: Conversation session. Fields: agent_id, channel_id, res_model, res_id, user_id, state (active, closed, expired), message_ids, summary, last_activity_date.
key_models.ai.tool: Tool definition. Fields: name, technical_name, description, schema (JSON), implementation_type (python, server_action), active, default_for_all.
key_models.ai.prompt: Prompt template. Fields: name, category, content (HTML with o_editor_prompt div), variable_ids, active, version, default_agent_ids.
key_models.ai_message: Message within an AI session. Fields: session_id, role (system, user, assistant, tool), content, tool_calls, tool_results, token_count, model_id, provider_id.
key_models.ai_agent_team_member: Agent-to-agent team membership. Fields: agent_id, member_agent_id, sequence, role, handoff_condition.

key_files.ai/models/ai_agent.py: Core ai.agent model with agent configuration and execution orchestration
key_files.ai/models/ai_provider.py: ai.provider model and provider-specific client initialization
key_files.ai/models/ai_model.py: ai.model registry with context window and pricing metadata
key_files.ai/models/ai_agent_session.py: Session lifecycle, history management, and summarization
key_files.ai/models/ai_tool.py: Tool registry, schema validation, and execution dispatcher
key_files.ai/models/ai_prompt.py: Prompt templates with o_editor_prompt HTML rendering
key_files.ai/security/security.xml: Groups and record rules for AI module

crons.ai_cron_session_cleanup: Archive expired sessions. Model: ai.agent.session, method: _cron_cleanup_expired_sessions
crons.ai_cron_usage_report: Generate usage/cost report. Model: ai.usage.log, method: _cron_send_usage_report
crons.ai_cron_summarize_long_sessions: Summarize sessions approaching context window. Model: ai.agent.session, method: _cron_summarize_long_sessions

security_groups.ai.group_ai_user: Use AI agents in channels and records they can access
security_groups.ai.group_ai_manager: Configure agents, prompts, and tools; manage deployments
security_groups.ai.group_ai_admin: Full access to providers, credentials, usage reports, and security settings
security_groups.base.group_system: Install AI module and access low-level configuration

record_rules.ai_agent_multi_company_rule: [('company_id','in',company_ids+[False])] — multi-company isolation for agents and providers
record_rules.ai_provider_credential_rule: Restricts API key read access to members of ai.group_ai_admin or explicit credential managers
record_rules.ai_session_personal_rule: [('|',('user_id','=',user.id),('user_id','=',False))] — users see own or unassigned sessions
record_rules.ai_session_channel_rule: Users can read sessions linked to channels they are members of
